A_concept.name: Recipes reference Ingredient Concept
A_concept.description: recipe_ingredients.ingredient_id → ingredients (concept). Costing via concept.default_pack_variant_id or derived current_price mirror.
A_concept.verdict: RECOMMENDED — aligns with Option E design
notes: ingredient_id FK preserved — semantic upgrade only
Costing reads default variant; optional line-level override in P3
Menu thinks in culinary terms not supplier SKUs

B_supplierProduct.name: Recipes reference Supplier Product
B_supplierProduct.description: recipe_ingredients.supplier_product_id → locks recipe to one supplier SKU
B_supplierProduct.verdict: REJECT for recipes — correct layer for purchasing only
notes: Menu recipes should not break when supplier switches
Would require recipe rewrite on every supplier change

C_packVariant.name: Recipes reference Pack Variant
C_packVariant.description: recipe_ingredients.pack_variant_id → locks recipe to specific purchase contract
C_packVariant.verdict: OPTIONAL override only — not primary binding
notes: Best for power users pinning exact pack (e.g. always Bocconcino tray)
Too granular for default menu recipe UX
Nullable pack_variant_id on line is forward-compatible add-on

recommendedModels.recipeIdentity: A — Ingredient Concept (current ingredient_id, future concept role)
recommendedModels.purchasingIdentity: D/E — Supplier Product → Pack Variant (invoice match, price_history, aliases)
recommendedModels.separationPrinciple: Recipes own culinary ontology; purchasing owns commercial contracts

recipeSchema.recipe_ingredients: ingredient_id XOR sub_recipe_id — already concept-level + prep graph
recipeSchema.prepSupport: sub_recipe_id FK recipes(id), output_quantity/output_unit on recipes
recipeSchema.uniqueConstraints: partial unique on (recipe_id, ingredient_id) and (recipe_id, sub_recipe_id)

currentCodebaseAlignment.identityCoupling: Costing reads ingredients.current_price — will become derived from default_pack_variant
currentCodebaseAlignment.oiCoupling: getRecipeMetrics uses same resolver — benefits from variant layer without schema change

mozzarella.detail: Recipe binds to concept 'Mozzarella'; Bocconcino vs Aviludo split is pack_variant + supplier_product. Recipe costing uses default_pack_variant_id (e.g. block for kitchen). Wrong cost today is purchasing overwrite on concept.current_price, not recipe FK.

pepino.detail: Recipe for pickles uses 'Pepino conserva' concept; fresh Bidfood line should not match that concept. Form guard / concept split is purchasing layer.

gingerBeer.detail: Recipe uses concept 'Ginger Beer'; volume SKU is pack_variant with volume_ml_per_unit. Extraction parse is purchasing normalization.

1_whatBreaksWhenVariantsIntroduced.answer: Minimal if recipes stay on concept: costing resolver must read default_pack_variant instead of ingredients.current_price. recipe_ingredients rows unchanged. Breaks only if recipes were bound to supplier_product (option B) or if code bypasses resolver and reads raw current_price.
breaks: resolve-operational-ingredient-cost.ts — redirect price source (adapter, not rewrite)

2_conceptRecipesVariantPurchases.answer: YES — this is the intended Option E separation and lowest migration risk path.

3_costingCorrectWhenPriceDerived.answer: YES when default_pack_variant_id is set and stable; RISK when multiple formats exist and default is wrong (VL Mozzarella: catalog €13.69 block vs kitchen using pieces).
3_costingCorrectWhenPriceDerived.mitigation: Per-concept default variant + optional recipe line pack_variant_id override (P3)

4_prepNowWithoutRewrites.answer: YES — prep/sub-recipe architecture is orthogonal to pack variants. sub_recipe_id, output_quantity, recipe-merge walk, prep propagation are already implemented.

5_technicalDebtIfRecipesNow.answer: LOW–MEDIUM if following concept binding + existing resolvers. HIGH only if binding recipes to supplier products or hardcoding ingredients.current_price outside resolver.
